content:
# Joshua Chapter 10 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 233-235 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_010_page_001.jpg`

---

**10:1** And-it-was when-heard Adoni-Zedek king of-Jerusalem that had-captured Joshua Ai and-had-devoted-it-to-destruction -- as-which he-did to-Jericho and-to-its-king, so he-did to-Ai and-to-its-king -- and-that had-made-peace [the]-inhabitants of-Gibeon with Israel and-were in-their-midst,

**10:2** and-they-feared greatly, because [was] a-city great Gibeon, as-one of-the-cities of-the-kingdom, and-because it [was] greater than the-Ai, and-all its-men [were] mighty-ones.

**10:3** And-sent Adoni-Zedek king of-Jerusalem to Hoham king of-Hebron, and-to Piram king of-Jarmuth, and-to Japhia king of-Lachish, and-to Debir king of-Eglon, saying,

**10:4** "Come-up to-me and-help-me, and-let-us-strike Gibeon, for it-has-made-peace with Joshua and-with [the]-sons of-Israel."

**10:5** And-they-gathered and-went-up, five kings of-the-Amorites -- king of-Jerusalem, king of-Hebron, king of-Jarmuth, king of-Lachish, king of-Eglon -- they and-all their-camps, and-they-encamped upon Gibeon, and-they-made-war against-it.

**10:6** And-sent [the]-men of-Gibeon to Joshua, to the-camp, to-the-Gilgal, saying, "Do-not-let-drop your-hands from-your-servants. Come-up to-us quickly and-save-us and-help-us, for have-gathered against-us all [the]-kings of-the-Amorites, [the]-inhabitants of-the-hill-country."

**10:7** And-went-up Joshua from the-Gilgal, he and-all [the]-people of-the-war with-him, and-all [the]-mighty-men of-the-valor.

**10:8** And-said YHWH to Joshua, "Do-not-fear them, for in-your-hand I-have-given-them; not shall-stand a-man of-them before-you."

**10:9** And-came to-them Joshua suddenly -- all the-night he-went-up from the-Gilgal.

**10:10** And-confused-them YHWH before Israel, and-he-struck-them [with]-a-striking great at-Gibeon, and-he-pursued-them [on the]-way of-[the]-ascent of-Beth-Horon, and-he-struck-them unto Azekah and-unto Makkedah.

**10:11** And-it-was in-their-fleeing from-before Israel -- they [were] on [the]-descent of-Beth-Horon -- and-YHWH cast upon-them stones great from the-heavens unto Azekah, and-they-died. More [were those] who died by-[the]-stones of-the-hail than [those] whom killed [the]-sons of-Israel by-the-sword.

**10:12** Then spoke Joshua to-YHWH in-the-day of-giving YHWH the-Amorites before [the]-sons of-Israel, and-he-said in-[the]-eyes of-Israel, "Sun, in-Gibeon be-still, and-moon, in-[the]-valley of-Aijalon."

**10:13** And-was-still the-sun, and-[the]-moon stood, until took-vengeance [the]-nation [on]-its-enemies. Is-not this written upon [the]-Book of-the-Upright? And-stood-still the-sun in-[the]-midst of-the-heavens, and-did-not-hasten to-go-in about a-day complete.

**10:14** And-there-was-not like-the-day the-that before-it and-after-it, for-hearing YHWH [the]-voice of-a-man, for YHWH [was] fighting for-Israel.

**10:15** And-returned Joshua, and-all Israel with-him, to the-camp, to-the-Gilgal.

**10:16** And-fled [the]-five kings the-these, and-they-hid in-the-cave at-Makkedah.

**10:17** And-it-was-told to-Joshua, saying, "Have-been-found [the]-five kings hiding in-the-cave at-Makkedah."

**10:18** And-said Joshua, "Roll stones great to [the]-mouth of-the-cave, and-appoint upon-it men to-guard-them.

**10:19** And-you, do-not-stand-still. Pursue after your-enemies, and-you-shall-strike-their-rear; do-not-allow-them to-enter into their-cities, for has-given-them YHWH your-God in-your-hand."

**10:20** And-it-was when-finished Joshua and-[the]-sons of-Israel to-strike-them [with]-a-striking very great, until they-were-consumed, and-the-survivors survived from-them, and-they-entered into the-cities of-the-fortifications.

**10:21** And-returned all the-people to the-camp, to Joshua, [at]-Makkedah in-peace; not sharpened a-man against-[the]-sons of-Israel his-tongue.

**10:22** And-said Joshua, "Open the-mouth of-the-cave, and-bring-out to-me the-five kings the-these from the-cave."

**10:23** And-they-did so, and-they-brought-out to-him the-five kings the-these from the-cave: the-king of-Jerusalem, the-king of-Hebron, the-king of-Jarmuth, the-king of-Lachish, the-king of-Eglon.

**10:24** And-it-was when-they-brought-out the-kings the-these to Joshua, and-called Joshua to-every man of-Israel, and-he-said to [the]-commanders of-[the]-men of-the-war who had-gone with-him, "Come-near, place your-feet upon [the]-necks of-the-kings the-these." And-they-came-near and-they-placed their-feet upon their-necks.

**10:25** And-said to-them Joshua, "Do-not-fear and-do-not-be-dismayed. Be-strong and-be-courageous, for thus shall-do YHWH to-all your-enemies whom you [are] fighting against-them."

**10:26** And-struck-them Joshua after-thus, and-he-put-them-to-death, and-he-hanged-them upon five trees, and-they-were hanging upon the-trees until the-evening.

**10:27** And-it-was at-[the]-time of-going of-the-sun, commanded Joshua, and-they-took-them-down from-upon the-trees, and-they-cast-them into the-cave where they-had-been-hiding there, and-they-placed stones great upon [the]-mouth of-the-cave, unto this the-day the-very.

**10:28** And Makkedah captured Joshua on-the-day the-that, and-he-struck-it by-[the]-mouth of-[the]-sword, and its-king he-devoted-to-destruction -- them and-every soul which [was] in-it; he-did-not-leave a-survivor. And-he-did to-[the]-king of-Makkedah as-which he-did to-[the]-king of-Jericho.

**10:29** And-passed-on Joshua, and-all Israel with-him, from-Makkedah to-Libnah, and-he-made-war against Libnah.

**10:30** And-gave YHWH also it in-[the]-hand of-Israel, and-its-king, and-he-struck-it by-[the]-mouth of-[the]-sword, and every soul which [was] in-it; he-did-not-leave in-it a-survivor. And-he-did to-its-king as-which he-did to-[the]-king of-Jericho.

**10:31** And-passed-on Joshua, and-all Israel with-him, from-Libnah to-Lachish, and-he-encamped upon-it, and-he-made-war against-it.

**10:32** And-gave YHWH Lachish in-[the]-hand of-Israel, and-he-captured-it on-the-day the-second, and-he-struck-it by-[the]-mouth of-[the]-sword, and every soul which [was] in-it, according-to-all which he-did to-Libnah.

**10:33** Then went-up Horam king of-Gezer to-help Lachish, and-struck-him Joshua and his-people, until he-did-not-leave to-him a-survivor.

**10:34** And-passed-on Joshua, and-all Israel with-him, from-Lachish to-Eglon, and-they-encamped upon-it, and-they-made-war against-it.

**10:35** And-they-captured-it on-the-day the-that, and-they-struck-it by-[the]-mouth of-[the]-sword, and every soul which [was] in-it on-the-day the-that he-devoted-to-destruction, according-to-all which he-did to-Lachish.

**10:36** And-went-up Joshua, and-all Israel with-him, from-Eglon to-Hebron, and-they-made-war against-it.

**10:37** And-they-captured-it and-they-struck-it by-[the]-mouth of-[the]-sword, and its-king and-all its-cities and-every soul which [was] in-it; he-did-not-leave a-survivor, according-to-all which he-did to-Eglon, and-he-devoted-it-to-destruction and-every soul which [was] in-it.

**10:38** And-returned Joshua, and-all Israel with-him, to-Debir, and-he-made-war against-it.

**10:39** And-he-captured-it and its-king and-all its-cities, and-they-struck-them by-[the]-mouth of-[the]-sword, and-they-devoted-to-destruction every soul which [was] in-it; he-did-not-leave a-survivor. As-which he-did to-Hebron, so he-did to-Debir and-to-its-king, and-as-which he-did to-Libnah and-to-its-king.

**10:40** And-struck Joshua all the-land: the-hill-country and-the-Negev and-the-lowland and-the-slopes, and all their-kings; he-did-not-leave a-survivor, and all the-breathing he-devoted-to-destruction, as-which commanded YHWH God of-Israel.

**10:41** And-struck-them Joshua from-Kadesh-Barnea and-unto Gaza, and all [the]-land of-Goshen and-unto Gibeon.

**10:42** And all the-kings the-these and their-land captured Joshua at-time one, for YHWH God of-Israel [was] fighting for-Israel.

**10:43** And-returned Joshua, and-all Israel with-him, to the-camp, to-the-Gilgal.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **10:1 -- Adoni-Zedek** (אֲדֹנִי־צֶדֶק): Literally "my-lord [is]-righteousness." The name is preserved untranslated as a proper noun. Compare with Melchi-Zedek (מַלְכִּי־צֶדֶק, "my-king [is]-righteousness") in Genesis 14:18.

2. **10:10 -- "confused-them"** (וַיְהֻמֵּם): From the root ה-מ-ם, indicating a panic or confusion sent by divine agency. The same root appears in the Exodus narratives of YHWH confounding enemies (Exodus 14:24, 23:27). The LXX (Vaticanus) renders this with ἐξέστησεν ("astounded/drove out of their senses").

3. **10:11 -- "stones of the hail"** (אַבְנֵי הַבָּרָד): The MT explicitly identifies these as hailstones, not merely "great stones." The word בָּרָד (barad) is the same word used for the plague of hail in Exodus 9:18-26, creating an intertextual echo of divine warfare.

4. **10:12-13 -- The Sun Standing Still**: The verb דּוֹם (dom, "be-still/silent") in v.12 is distinct from עָמַד (amad, "stood/stood-still") in v.13. Joshua commands the sun to "be-silent" (cease its activity), and the text then reports it "stood" in the midst of the heavens. The moon is commanded separately regarding the Valley of Aijalon. The citation from the "Book of the Upright" (סֵפֶר הַיָּשָׁר, Sefer HaYashar) indicates an older poetic source.

5. **10:13 -- "about a day complete"** (כְּיוֹם תָּמִים): The preposition כְּ (ke, "about/like") with תָּמִים (tamim, "complete/whole") suggests an approximation -- "about a whole day" rather than an exact measure.

6. **10:14 -- "for-hearing YHWH [the]-voice of-a-man"**: The Hebrew לִשְׁמֹעַ יְהוָה בְּקוֹל אִישׁ uses the infinitive construct with ל, indicating the purpose or cause: the uniqueness of this day was that YHWH listened to a human voice [to alter the cosmic order].

7. **10:21 -- "not sharpened a-man... his-tongue"** (לֹא־חָרַץ... אִישׁ לִבְנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל אֶת־לְשֹׁנוֹ): Literally "no man sharpened his tongue against the sons of Israel." This idiom (cf. Exodus 11:7) means no one dared to speak against them -- complete intimidation of all opposition.

8. **10:24 -- "commanders of the men of the war"** (קְצִינֵי אַנְשֵׁי הַמִּלְחָמָה): The word קָצִין (qatsin) denotes a military officer or commander, distinct from שַׂר (sar, "prince/chief"). The foot-on-neck gesture is an ancient Near Eastern symbol of complete subjugation, attested in Egyptian and Mesopotamian iconography.

9. **10:27 -- "unto this the-day the-very"** (עַד־עֶצֶם הַיּוֹם הַזֶּה): The word עֶצֶם (etsem) literally means "bone/substance/very," intensifying "this day" -- "unto the very substance of this day." This formulaic phrase is a marker of the narrator's own time.

10. **10:28-39 -- The Southern Campaign Formula**: The repeated phrase "struck-it by-the-mouth of-the-sword" (וַיַּכֶּהָ לְפִי־חֶרֶב) uses פִּי (pi, "mouth"), creating the vivid image of the sword "devouring" as if with a mouth. The formula is applied systematically to Makkedah, Libnah, Lachish, Eglon, Hebron, and Debir.

11. **10:40 -- "the-breathing"** (כָּל־הַנְּשָׁמָה): From נְשָׁמָה (neshamah), the same word used in Genesis 2:7 for the "breath of life" that YHWH breathed into Adam. The חֵרֶם (herem, "devotion to destruction") of all neshamah carries profound theological weight -- the breath given by God is reclaimed by God's decree.

12. **10:40 -- "the-slopes"** (הָאֲשֵׁדוֹת): This refers to the eastern slopes descending toward the Arabah/Jordan Valley. The four geographical terms (hill-country, Negev, lowland, slopes) constitute the complete topographical zones of the southern Judean territory.

13. **LXX Variants (Vaticanus)**: The LXX largely follows the MT in this chapter, though the Vaticanus manuscript shows some differences in the list of conquered cities and occasionally condenses the repetitive campaign formula. In v.15, the LXX omits the return to Gilgal (which many scholars consider a scribal anticipation of v.43), though the MT retains it. EMET follows the MT as the primary text.
